hazards.source: Icelandic Meteorological Office & University of Iceland Climate Projections 2023
hazards.description: Rising sea levels combined with increased storm surge frequency pose a significant threat to low-lying coastal areas.

hazards.source: National Power Company of Iceland (Landsvirkjun) Grid Resilience Study
hazards.description: Climate models predict an increase in the frequency and intensity of severe North Atlantic winter storms.

hazards.source: Reykjavik Public Works Department, Winter Maintenance Report
hazards.description: The city is experiencing more frequent intense snowfall events and ice storm conditions.

goals.description: Protect 90% of critical coastal infrastructure and residential areas from a 1-in-100-year storm surge event through a combination of hard and soft engineering solutions.
Enhance the resilience of the city's power grid and building stock to withstand wind speeds up to 45 m/s.
Improve winter maintenance capacity to ensure all primary and secondary roads are cleared within 12 hours of a major snowfall event.

actions.title: Reykjavik Coastal Resilience Program - Phase 1: Sea Wall & Dune Restoration
Grid Hardening and Undergrounding Feasibility Study

cases.question: What percentage of the population is exposed to hazards?
Are there any goals set for the year 2050?
